7: IsMeasurementSystem.sum_one.{u_1, u_2} {R : Type u_1} [Ring R] [StarRing R] {I : Type u_2} [Fintype I] {f : I → R} (self : IsMeasurementSystem f) : ∑ x, f x = 1
6: The universe of propositions. `Prop ≡ Sort 0`. Every proposition is propositionally equal to either `True` or `False`.
3: Fintype.{u_4} (α : Type u_4) : Type u_4`Fintype α` means that `α` is finite, i.e. there are only finitely many distinct elements of type `α`. The evidence of this is a finset `elems` (a list up to permutation without duplicates), together with a proof that everything of type `α` is in the list.
20: A type universe. `Type ≡ Type 0`, `Type u ≡ Sort (u + 1)`.
2: The syntax `variable (X Y ... Z : Type*)` creates a new distinct implicit universe variable `> 0` for each variable in the sequence.
18: Fin (n : ℕ) : TypeNatural numbers less than some upper bound. In particular, a `Fin n` is a natural number `i` with the constraint that `i < n`. It is the canonical type with `n` elements.
13: Star.star.{u} {R : Type u} [self : Star R] : R → RA star operation (e.g. complex conjugate).
12: IsMeasurementSystem.self_adjoint.{u_1, u_2} {R : Type u_1} [Ring R] [StarRing R] {I : Type u_2} [Fintype I] {f : I → R} (self : IsMeasurementSystem f) (x : I) : star (f x) = f x
11: IsMeasurementSystem.orthogonal.{u_1, u_2} {R : Type u_1} [Ring R] [StarRing R] {I : Type u_2} [Fintype I] {f : I → R} (self : IsMeasurementSystem f) (x y : I) : x ≠ y → f x * f y = 0
